evals.name: e2e-cross-mode-no-contamination
evals.prompt: /work-on -- three sequential invocations in one session: (1) /work-on #42 (2) /work-on "fix the login timeout" (3) /work-on docs/plans/PLAN-my-feature.md -- describe the template and vars used for each
evals.expected_output: For (1) issue-backed: koto init with work-on.md, ISSUE_NUMBER=42, ARTIFACT_PREFIX=issue_42, mode: issue_backed. For (2) free-form: koto init with work-on.md, ARTIFACT_PREFIX=task_fix-login-timeout, mode: free_form, no ISSUE_NUMBER. For (3) plan mode: koto init with work-on-plan.md, PLAN_DOC=docs/plans/PLAN-my-feature.md. Each invocation uses distinct template and vars with no state from prior invocations.
expectations: For invocation (1) issue-backed, agent plans to use work-on.md template with ISSUE_NUMBER=42 and ARTIFACT_PREFIX=issue_42
For invocation (2) free-form, agent plans to use work-on.md template with ARTIFACT_PREFIX derived from task slug but no ISSUE_NUMBER
For invocation (3) plan mode, agent plans to use work-on-plan.md template (not work-on.md) with PLAN_DOC var
Agent does not reuse or carry over ISSUE_NUMBER, ARTIFACT_PREFIX, or template from one invocation to the next
Each mode submits correct entry evidence: issue_backed uses mode: issue_backed; free_form uses mode: free_form; plan mode initializes plan orchestrator

evals.name: plan-completion-cascade
evals.prompt: After pr_finalization completes for a plan orchestration, what does the plan_completion state do?
evals.expected_output: Agent invokes skills/work-on/scripts/run-cascade.sh --push {{PLAN_DOC}}, reads cascade_status from the JSON output, marks the PR ready via gh pr ready, and submits cascade_status to koto. The cascade script runs the chain-targeted lifecycle check internally at the pre-cascade probe and post-cascade verification points; the agent does not invoke the validator directly. The cascade runs BEFORE gh pr ready so CI re-runs on the ready_for_review event in strict mode against the finalized chain.
expectations: Agent invokes run-cascade.sh --push {{PLAN_DOC}} as the cascade step rather than executing the cascade steps individually
Agent does NOT invoke shirabe validate --lifecycle . --strict directly; the cascade script runs the chain-targeted lifecycle check internally
Agent marks the PR ready via gh pr ready as the final step of plan_completion (after the cascade lands)
Agent reads cascade_status from the JSON output emitted by run-cascade.sh
Agent submits cascade_status to koto (completed, partial, or skipped)
Agent does NOT manually git rm the PLAN doc, run shirabe transition, or update ROADMAP entries directly

evals.expected_output: Agent receives the pr_creation state with SHARED_BRANCH set. Recognizes this child is running on the orchestrator's shared branch where the orchestrator owns the PR. Submits pr_status: shared. Workflow routes directly to done without visiting ci_monitor.
expectations: Agent submits pr_status: shared from pr_creation state
Agent does NOT attempt to create a new PR when SHARED_BRANCH is indicated

evals.name: ci-monitor-dirty-merge-state-routing
evals.expected_output: The plan-orchestrator reaches ci_monitor on the shared impl/foo branch. `gh pr view --json mergeStateStatus --jq .mergeStateStatus` returns DIRTY (the PR has merge conflicts and GitHub has suppressed new check-runs). The merge_state_clean gate fails. The ci_passing gate command evaluates length == 0 against zero check-runs and returns true — the agent does NOT loop on ci_passing because this is the silent-success surface #162 names. Instead the agent submits ci_outcome: dirty_merge_state with rationale naming the conflict files. The workflow routes to escalate_dirty_merge_state → done_blocked carrying the DIRTY-specific failure_reason 'ci_monitor: PR merge state is DIRTY; checks suppressed.' The operator's recovery is to rebase the shared branch and resolve conflicts before re-running CI; the koto state machine does not retry automatically.
expectations: Agent observes gh pr view --json mergeStateStatus returning DIRTY
Agent submits ci_outcome: dirty_merge_state (NOT ci_outcome: passing, even though ci_passing gate returns length == 0)
Agent's rationale names the conflict files for operator recovery
Workflow routes to escalate_dirty_merge_state → done_blocked
failure_reason includes 'ci_monitor: PR merge state is DIRTY; checks suppressed.'
The koto state machine does not retry automatically; the operator must rebase

evals.name: worktree-discipline-intent-changing-escalates
evals.expected_output: The plan-orchestrator reaches worktree_discipline_check after orchestrator_setup. The agent runs `git fetch origin && git rebase origin/main`, classifies the upstream impact per references/worktree-discipline.md, and writes wip/work-on_bar_impact.json. The impact is intent-changing: main has advanced in a way that invalidates the PLAN's intent (e.g., the design's referenced files were deleted, or the contract the PLAN relies on was changed). The agent submits impact: intent-changing with a rationale. The workflow routes to escalate_upstream_drift → done_blocked carrying the upstream-drift-specific failure_reason. Children are NEVER dispatched because the per-child loop runs only after the worktree_discipline_check classifies impact as none or informational. This validates R8/R9: upstream drift is caught mid-chain rather than at PR finalization.
expectations: Agent fetches origin and rebases on main before classifying impact
Agent writes wip/work-on_<PLAN_SLUG>_impact.json with the impact classification
Agent submits impact: intent-changing with a rationale (rationale required for this branch)
Workflow routes to escalate_upstream_drift → done_blocked (children NOT dispatched)
failure_reason includes the rationale and the worktree_discipline_check origin
R8/R9 preserved: upstream drift caught mid-chain, not at PR finalization

evals.name: worktree-discipline-none-and-informational-proceed
evals.expected_output: The plan-orchestrator reaches worktree_discipline_check after orchestrator_setup. The agent runs git fetch + rebase, classifies impact as either none (main has not advanced or advanced in directories the PLAN does not touch) or informational (advanced but the changes don't invalidate the PLAN's intent — e.g., docs-only edits, unrelated test additions). The agent submits impact: none (or impact: informational) and the workflow routes forward to spawn_and_await without escalation. The classification is recorded in wip/work-on_<PLAN_SLUG>_impact.json for audit. The per-child dispatch then proceeds normally.
expectations: Agent submits impact: none OR impact: informational
Workflow routes to spawn_and_await (per-child dispatch proceeds)
wip/work-on_<PLAN_SLUG>_impact.json records the classification for audit
No escalation occurs on none or informational impact

evals.name: coordinated-effort-syncs-as-per-repo-prs-progress
evals.prompt: /work-on docs/plans/PLAN-multi-repo.md -- the PLAN's execution_mode is coordinated
evals.expected_output: Agent detects PLAN.md input with execution_mode: coordinated and runs the plan orchestrator in coordination-aware mode. As per-repo PRs open and progress, the agent calls shirabe coordination sync on each orchestrator pass to recompute the durable PR-index and the two-node merge-order from live gh, and calls shirabe validate --merge-gate as the merge-last check. These are smart defaults: each ANNOUNCES itself in the invocation output, naming the behavior and its per-invocation override per R18 (e.g. on the first sync, announce that the index/merge-order is recomputed live). The shirabe coordination verb args and their fail-closed behavior are owned by the CLI; the agent binds to references/coordination-strategy.md and does not restate the contract.
expectations: Agent detects execution_mode: coordinated and runs the plan orchestrator in coordination-aware mode
Agent plans to call shirabe coordination sync on each orchestrator pass as per-repo PRs open and progress, recomputing the durable index and two-node merge-order from live gh
Agent plans to call shirabe validate --merge-gate as the merge-last check before the coordination PR can merge
Agent ANNOUNCES the sync/gate smart defaults in the invocation output, naming the per-invocation override per R18
Agent binds to ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/references/coordination-strategy.md and does NOT restate the contract or the verb fail-closed behavior
Agent does NOT write across a repo boundary; the cross-repo boundary is the read-only verification gate

evals.name: coordinated-merge-last-gate-blocks-while-pr-unmerged
evals.prompt: /work-on docs/plans/PLAN-multi-repo.md -- coordinated effort where one indexed per-repo PR is still open
evals.expected_output: Agent runs shirabe validate --merge-gate, which recomputes merge state from authoritative live gh api queries at gate time (never by parsing the editable PR body, F4). One indexed per-repo PR is still open, so the merge-last gate fails and the coordination PR is kept unmerged. The gate fails closed: any PR it cannot resolve is treated as not-merged. The non-bypassable lifecycle.yml strict-mode check (draft == false trigger) is the backstop that keeps the coordination PR unmerged while any indexed PR is open or finalization is incomplete. A coordination step that cannot complete halts and surfaces the error rather than advancing (R21). The agent does not merge the coordination PR while any indexed PR is unmerged.
expectations: Agent plans to run shirabe validate --merge-gate, recomputing merge state from live gh api queries at gate time, never from the editable PR body (F4)
Agent recognizes the merge-last gate FAILS while any indexed per-repo PR is unmerged, keeping the coordination PR unmerged
Agent understands the gate fails closed: any unresolvable PR is treated as not-merged
Agent identifies the non-bypassable lifecycle.yml strict-mode check (draft == false trigger) as the merge-last backstop
Agent halts and surfaces the error on a coordination step that cannot complete rather than advancing (R21)
Agent does NOT merge the coordination PR while any indexed PR is unmerged or finalization is incomplete

evals.name: coordination-intent-absent-behavior-unchanged-r3
evals.prompt: /work-on docs/plans/PLAN-single-repo.md -- the PLAN is single-pr or multi-pr (no coordination intent)
evals.expected_output: The PLAN's execution_mode is single-pr or multi-pr and no coordination intent is signaled (no --coordinated flag, no coordinated-default header). Per R3 the coordination capability is ADDITIVE: the plan orchestrator behaves exactly as before -- one repo, one shared branch, one PR, no coordination sync, no cross-repo gate. The agent does NOT call shirabe coordination create or sync, or shirabe validate --merge-gate, and does NOT add coordination prompts or a merge-last gate. The coordination-aware section of the SKILL applies only when the PLAN is coordinated (or intent is otherwise signaled).
expectations: Agent resolves coordination intent as ABSENT (execution_mode single-pr or multi-pr, no --coordinated flag, no coordinated-default header)
Agent runs the plan orchestrator exactly as before: one repo, one shared branch, one PR
Agent does NOT call shirabe coordination create or sync, or shirabe validate --merge-gate when intent is absent
Agent does NOT add coordination prompts, cross-repo gate, or merge-last behavior on the intent-absent path
Agent confirms R3 additivity: the coordination capability changes nothing for a non-coordinated PLAN
